summary.intent: Address a third Codex review pass on PR #681 (fogodev/ars-ui) by fixing four new findings in the TimeField component: composition keydown suppression, stale timer cancellation on SetValue, localized forced-width digits, and non-panicking id resync.
summary.outcome: All four regression tests written (red-green TDD), production fixes applied to mod.rs, spec updated, formatting/clippy/coverage/proptest/mutation all clean (0 missed mutants out of 248), and changes committed with Codex co-author trailer on branch t3code/2ddd4539.

repo: Codex review state is tracked via GitHub issue reactions on the PR: 👀 means actively reviewing, 👍 means approved.
The repo has a skill at .agents/skills/waiting-for-codex-review/SKILL.md that defines the polling loop protocol for Codex review passes.

DateField has pre-existing behavior matching several of the TimeField issues (is_composing suppression, id sync) but fixes were intentionally scoped to TimeField only to avoid unreviewed DateField regressions.
cargo xtask spec validate is the gate for spec-conformance checks; cargo test --test spec_conformance runs the spec-derived test suite.
Mutation testing uses cargo-mutants with --iterate and targets a single file; partial results accumulate in mutants.out/ (missed.txt, caught.txt, unviable.txt, timeout.txt).

code.finding: The `format` import became unused after routing forced-width digits through the i18n backend; it had to be removed to satisfy clippy -D warnings.
The IntlBackend stub in test helpers requires hour_cycle and week_info fields in addition to base locale fields; omitting them causes a compile error that masks the intended red-phase test failures.
IntlBackend stub structure — referenced when diagnosing the test helper compile gap for localized digit formatting.

workflow: Write failing regression tests before touching production code (red-green TDD); confirms each fix addresses exactly the intended behavior rather than accidentally passing.
When cargo-mutants seems stalled, inspect mutants.out/ directly with find + wc -l on partial result files to distinguish a slow run from a hung process without killing it.
cargo llvm-cov test --summary-only scoped to a module gives a fast coverage sanity check without a full instrumented build.
cargo +nightly fmt --all --check before committing catches test-helper formatting that rustfmt doesn't fix automatically during editing.

friction: Codex review took ~20 minutes of 120s polling loops before returning four new threads — unavoidable latency but adds significant wall-clock time per review pass.
Test helper compile gap (IntlBackend missing hour_cycle and week_info) blocked the initial red-phase run, requiring a patch before the TDD flow could begin.
Unused `format` import surfaced only at the spec-conformance build step, not during the unit test run, requiring a separate fix pass after the main production patch.
cargo-mutants ran for an unusually long time on 248 mutants, requiring a defensive check of the mutants.out/ directory to confirm the process was still making progress.
open_items: DateField has older behavior matching the same four TimeField issues (composition suppression, SetValue timer cancellation, forced-width digit formatting, id resync); these were explicitly deferred and not fixed in this session.
CI matrix jobs (broader platform/feature matrix beyond the focused checks) were still running at commit time — full green CI not yet confirmed.
A fourth Codex review pass will be needed after the push; the session ends before that review cycle completes.
